# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

GLua addon for Garry's Mod that implements a functional TARDIS. Runs inside the GMod engine — there is no compile step, no test runner, and `lua`/`luac` are not available locally. CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) only packages and publishes to the Steam Workshop. Runtime depends on the sibling [Doors](https://github.com/AmyJeanes/Doors) addon (see `../Doors`).

## Auto-generated files — do not hand-edit

These are generated by PowerShell scripts and overwritten in CI - don't hand-edit them:

- `lua/tardis/languages/*.lua` ← `i18n/languages/*.json`, via `pwsh -File scripts/generate-languages.ps1` (`generate-files.yml`). Only edit `i18n/languages/en.json` by hand; other locales are filled in by the script (calls OpenAI when `TARDIS_OPENAI_API_KEY` is set, otherwise leaves blanks).
- `lua/tardis/libraries/libraries/libraries/sh_version_generated.lua` ← git state (nearest release tag, commit count, sha), via `pwsh -File scripts/generate-version.ps1`. **Gitignored, not committed** - it records the commit sha, so committing it would change the sha it records. CI generates it at pack time (`scripts/pre-pack.ps1`, run by the shared publish workflow); `install-tools.ps1` generates it locally. A checkout without it falls back to channel `source` and no version, so don't be surprised by `0.0.0` in a fresh clone.
- The `---@overload` block above each entity's `ENT:AddHook` (in `lua/entities/gmod_tardis*/shared.lua`) ← every `CallHook("Name", ...)` site, via `pwsh -File scripts/generate-hook-types.ps1` (`generate-hook-types.yml`). It types each hook callback's payload params, so `ENT:AddHook("PlayerEnter", "id", function(self, ply) ... end)` types `ply` with no manual `---@param`. To add or fix a payload type, retype it at the `CallHook` call site (e.g. the `colData` on `ENT:PhysicsCollide`); a hook whose args the scraper can't resolve falls to `any`, so keep a manual `---@param` on that one callback if a concrete type matters.

## API reference wiki (`scripts/generate-wiki-api.ps1`)

The type-reference pages in the sibling `TARDIS.wiki` repo are generated from the `---@class` / `---@field` annotations on the content-authoring classes (interiors, exteriors, parts, controls, sequences, settings, tips, icon packs, GUI themes, screens). `emmylua_doc_cli` (the same EmmyLua engine as `glua_ls`) parses the annotations into a JSON type model; the renderer emits one markdown page per category between `<!-- BEGIN/END GENERATED API REFERENCE -->` markers, preserving the hand-written intro above them. The generator engine (`Invoke-WikiGen`) and the headless-defaults harness live in the shared [`gmod-addon-tools`](https://github.com/AmyJeanes/gmod-addon-tools) module (cloned beside this addon; see First-time setup); `scripts/generate-wiki-api.ps1` is a thin driver supplying TARDIS's defaults provider, while `scripts/wiki-api.config.ps1` supplies the reusable category/owned-prefix config other generated-wiki addons can discover. CI: `generate-wiki.yml`, on push to `dev` touching `lua/**`. So **those annotations ARE the wiki** - write them to read well. Field types render exactly as the analyzer resolves them (so they match editor hover); a type with `|nil` / trailing `?` is shown as optional. Cross-addon type links are discovered from `.luarc.json` workspace libraries only when that sibling addon exposes its own `scripts/wiki-api.config.ps1`.

Doc-comment attachment is strict (verified against `emmylua_doc_cli`); follow these or descriptions silently land on the wrong field, or fields vanish entirely:

- **Class description**: a `---` doc comment on the line directly above `---@class` (no blank line between).
- **Field description**: trailing on the field - `---@field name type the description` - or a `---` doc comment on the line directly above `---@field`. Pick one style per field; don't put trailing text *and* a following `---` block on the same field (the block appends to the preceding field, not the next).
- **Never put a blank line inside a `---@class` block.** One blank line orphans every `---@field` below it - they silently drop off the class, breaking both type checking and the wiki. Keep the whole `---@class` ... `---@field` run contiguous.
- The analyzer is lenient: a plain `--` comment directly above a `---@class`/`---@field` is picked up the same as `---`, so use `---` for intended docs. A code-only comment above a *class* can be detached with a blank line; one *inside* a field block can't (the blank line would orphan the fields), so it becomes the next field's description - keep code-only notes out of the field block.

## File-loading convention

`lua/autorun/tardis.lua` and the `LoadFolder` methods on each entity drive everything. A file's prefix decides where it runs:

- `sh_*` — shared (included on both, `AddCSLuaFile` on server)
- `cl_*` — client only
- `sv_*` — server only
- Anything else — only loaded when `LoadFolder(..., noprefix=true)` is used (parts, controls, interiors)

Top-level load order is intentional and stacked so deeper libraries register first:
`libraries/libraries/libraries` → `libraries/libraries` → `libraries` → top-level `tardis/`. Adding a library that something else depends on means putting it deep enough that it loads first.

## Architecture: exterior + interior + parts

Three SENT bases form the spine:

- `gmod_tardis` (exterior, extends the Doors base entity `gmod_door_exterior`) — `lua/entities/gmod_tardis/`
- `gmod_tardis_interior` (extends the Doors base entity `gmod_door_interior`) — `lua/entities/gmod_tardis_interior/`
- `gmod_tardis_part` — every interactable (console, doors, levers, monitors)

Both the exterior and interior `shared.lua` install a per-class hook system (`ENT:AddHook(name, id, func)` / `ENT:CallHook(name, ...)`) and then `LoadFolder("modules/libraries")` followed by `LoadFolder("modules")`. Each module file in `lua/entities/gmod_tardis*/modules/` registers behavior by hooking, e.g. `ENT:AddHook("PlayerInitialize", "interior", function(self) ... end)`. The first hook to return non-nil short-circuits the chain. `CallCommonHook` from either side cascades exterior → interior.

Cross-side calls go via `:CallClientHook` / `:CallClientCommonHook`, which `net.SendMessage` over to the matching `OnMessage("client_hook", ...)` handler.

Custom hooks can also be attached at the **metadata** level (`metadata.Exterior.CustomHooks`, `metadata.Interior.CustomHooks`, and `metadata.CustomHooks` with `inthooks`/`exthooks` flags) — `CallHook` walks these after the registered hooks. Anything reading `self.metadata` should expect this layered structure.

## Parts (`lua/tardis/parts/` and `lua/tardis/sh_parts.lua`)

Parts are registered with `TARDIS:AddPart(PART_TABLE)`. **Part IDs must be lowercase** (enforced with an error). Each `AddPart` call ends up registering a SENT named `gmod_tardis_part_<id>` whose `Draw`, `Initialize`, `Think`, `Use`, `OnRemove` are wrapped by `sh_parts.lua` to handle:

- Animation (`Animate`, `AnimateOptions`/`ExtraAnimations`, types: `toggle` / `perpetual_use` / `travel` / `idle` / `custom`) driven by `TARDIS.ProcessAnimation`.
- Portal-aware draw culling (`InteriorPart` / `ExteriorPart` / `AllowThroughPortals` / `DrawThroughPortal`).
- Sound on toggle (`Sound`, `SoundOn`, `SoundOff`, `SoundNoPower`, `SoundLoop`, …) — `TARDIS:TogglePart` picks the right one from current power state.
- Auto physics setup (`AutoSetup = true`).

Per-interior part overrides live in metadata (`metadata.Interior.Parts[id]` / `metadata.Exterior.Parts[id]`) and are merged into the part instance during `SetupParts`. A part bound to a control (via `metadata.Interior.Controls[partID] = "control_id"`) will route its `Use` through `TARDIS:Control(control_id, ply, self)` instead of calling its own `Use`.

## Controls (`lua/tardis/controls/sh_control_*.lua`)

Controls are abstract actions (`door`, `power`, `flight`, `teleport`, …) registered via `TARDIS:AddControl({ id=..., ext_func=..., int_func=..., serveronly|clientonly, screen_button=..., tip_text=... })`. They are invoked from a player's TARDIS via `TARDIS:Control(id, ply, part)`, which dispatches the `ext_func` / `int_func` and networks via the `TARDIS-Control` message when called from the client. `control.moves` registers automatic part toggles in response to engine hooks (see `sh_control.lua:CallControlMove`).

When adding a control: also wire it into the relevant interior's metadata `Controls` table, and (if it should appear on screens) populate `screen_button`. Translation strings live in `i18n/languages/en.json` under `Controls.*`.

## Interiors / metadata (`lua/tardis/metadata/`, `lua/tardis/interiors/`)

`base.lua` defines defaults; concrete interiors (`default.lua`, etc.) set `T.Base = "base"` and extend. `TARDIS:AddInterior(t)` stores raw tables; `TARDIS:CreateInteriorMetadata(id, ent)` lazily merges through `Base` chains and applies templates (`lua/tardis/interiors/templates/`) and exterior overrides (`lua/tardis/interiors/exteriors/`). The merge has a quirk specific to `Teleport` sequences (`DematSequence`/`MatSequence`/`HadsDematSequence`) — `PreMergeExteriorMetadata` / `PostMergeExteriorMetadata` swap them to `*Saved` and back so `table.Merge` doesn't deep-merge them; preserve that behaviour if touching merge logic.

Validation in `ValidateMetadata` — adding new `Min`/`Max` boxes? Mirror the existing checks.

## Tooling

- `.luarc.json` configures sumneko-LuaLS with `./.tools/glua-api` (GLua type stubs) plus sibling addons (`../Doors`, `../world-portals`, `../wire`, `../Sonic-Screwdriver`) on the workspace library. The recommended VS Code extension is `Pollux.gmod-glua-ls`.
- `wire` Expression 2 integration lives under `lua/entities/gmod_wire_expression2/` and is excluded from LuaLS analysis.

### Removing `---@diagnostic disable` rules from `.luarc.json`

The `disable` block is now **empty** — every flow-analysis rule is enforced. It had been re-added wholesale on the glua_ls 1.0.15 → 1.0.27 bump (1.0.20+'s flow-based nil analysis and stricter inference flooded ~400 false positives on TARDIS's dynamic `self` dispatch, runtime-set entity fields, and integer-enum usage — zero real bugs) and was whittled back to nothing rule by rule. The per-rule notes below record how each was satisfied; keep new code matching these patterns.

Per-rule status after a full triage of every diagnostic:

- `assign-type-mismatch` — **enabled.** 5 false positives, all fixed: 4 `Trace.mask = MASK_NPCWORLDSTATIC` (handled by the enum constant re-typing in `.luatypes/glua_overrides.lua`, see `param-type-mismatch`), 1 `screen.id` string marker the analyzer mistypes as integer from sibling-addon `id` structs (one-line `disable-next-line`).
- `param-type-mismatch` — **enabled.** Was 42 false positives, zero real bugs. 38 integer-enum (`DOCK`/`MASK`/`COLLISION_GROUP`/`DMG`/render-target enums) fixed by re-typing each constant as its alias in `.luatypes/glua_overrides.lua` (`---@type DOCK` on `TOP` etc.) — the pattern Doors/world-portals use. On 1.0.27 the constant re-typing is the *only* mechanism that works: alias re-declaration (`---@alias DOCK integer`) and `@overload` widening both do nothing. Add a line there when a new strictly-typed enum constant gets used. The 4 inference quirks fixed in source: `parseTriple` now returns a single `number[]` table instead of `nil, errmsg`-or-`x, y, z` (the error-string return was polluting the coordinate type), and `TARDIS.bindkeys` keys are typed `string`.
- `undefined-field` — **enabled.** Was 84 false positives, zero real bugs, all fixed source-side by typing the dynamic `self`/tables. `gmod_tardis_part` is the one part type: the SENT's `shared.lua` declares `---@class gmod_tardis_part : Entity` with the runtime links (`exterior`/`interior`), and `sh_parts.lua` *re-declares* `---@class gmod_tardis_part` (same name - the analyzer merges them) with every PART-definition field and the *non-engine* override hooks (`UseBasic`/`PreDraw`/`PostDraw`). The engine ENTITY hooks (`Initialize`/`Think`/`Use`/`Draw`/`OnRemove`) come from `Entity`, so they're not redeclared. How a part is typed depends on whether it adds its own state:
    - **Plain parts** (only entity methods + `gmod_tardis_part` fields - e.g. `vortex`, `events`, `parts_default_basic`, `parts_default_controls`): write `local PART = TARDIS:NewPart()` (and `PART = TARDIS:NewPart()` for each reassignment). `NewPart` is annotated `---@return gmod_tardis_part`, so `PART` - and therefore `self` in every method - is typed with no per-file class and no casts. A `---@return`-typed value isn't literal-checked, so unlike `--[[@as gmod_tardis_part]]` it never trips `missing-fields`; it also gives stricter field-assignment checking than an `@as` cast (it caught `SoundNoPower = false` vs `string`, which is why that field is `string|false|nil`). `self` is typed *only* by `PART`'s type - declaring a method on the class does not type `self` in a plain `function PART:Method()` (tested) - so `NewPart()` (or a subclass) is what does the work.
    - **Parts with their own runtime fields/methods** (`door`, `intdoor`, `monitors`): `---@class part_<id> : gmod_tardis_part` on `local PART={}` (a plain `{}`, not `NewPart()`). The analyzer infers the custom `self.X` fields onto that subclass, so methods need no cast. Multi-PART such files (`monitors`) also need `--[[@as part_<id>]]` on each `PART = {}` *reassignment* (a bare reassignment drops the type, and `--[[@as X]]` only skips the `missing-fields` check for a class declared on a `local t={}`, not for the merged `gmod_tardis_part` itself). Only add a subclass when a part genuinely has custom state - most don't.
    - **Metatable-OOP classes** (`HexagonalLayout`, `ListView3D`): a `---@class` with the `:new` fields plus `---@return <Class>` on the constructor.
    - **Dynamic-key tables** get `---@type table<K, V>`; a function whose result is indexed dynamically gets `---@return table<K, V>` so every call site inherits it.
  A new PART field referenced via `self` goes on the `gmod_tardis_part` block in `sh_parts.lua` (or the per-file subclass if part-specific, e.g. monitors' `MonitorID`).
- `unchecked-nil-access` — **enabled.** Was 119, zero real bugs, all fixed with **zero casts** (no `---@cast`/`@as`) — source-side types only. Root cause: in 1.0.27 a field access on a value typed as the bare `table` is itself nilable, so a *chained* access (`metadata.Interior.X.Y`, member-of / index / method-call) flags even when nothing is nil (a leaf read is fine). The fix is to give every structured table a real class so the chain resolves. Patterns, in preference order:
    - **Strong-type the schema — fully, including fields no shipped interior uses.** The metadata is a complete class hierarchy in `sh_metadata.lua`: `tardis_metadata` → `tardis_interior_metadata` / `tardis_exterior_metadata`, and *every* inner structured field is its own class too — `tardis_interior_sound_metadata`/`tardis_exterior_sound_metadata` (→ `tardis_sound_damage`/`_door`/`_teleport`/`_power`), `tardis_teleport`, `tardis_versions`, `tardis_portal`, `tardis_box` (Size/ExitBox), `tardis_fallback`, `tardis_light` (+ `NoLO`/`NoExtra`/`NoExtraNoLO` variants), `tardis_light_override`, `tardis_projected_light`, `tardis_chameleon`, `tardis_locked_door`, `tardis_tip_settings`, `tardis_timings`, plus `tardis_tip`/`tardis_tip_colors`/`tardis_tip_colorset` (in `cl_tips.lua`) and `tardis_part_original` (in `sh_parts.lua`). Source the field set from **`base.lua` (the canonical schema) + every interior/exterior/template definition + all accesses** — not just `default.lua`; grep `\.Interior\.\w+` / `\.Exterior\.\w+` for reads and `^\s*(T|E)\.\w+\s*=` in `interiors/` for top-level keys. Attach the class to the `Metadata`/`MetadataRaw`/`MetadataTemplates`/`MetadataVersions`/`ExteriorsMetadata` storage, the `metadata` field on both entity classes, the `tips` field on `gmod_tardis_interior`, and the returns of `CreateInteriorMetadata`/`MergeTemplates`/`MergeMetadata`/`CreateExteriorMetadata`. A sub-field that gets *chained* through must be non-optional; **every leaf-read sub-field must also be declared or it trips `undefined-field`** — converge by rescanning. Interior `Exterior` section vs the standalone exterior from `AddExterior` share `tardis_exterior_metadata` (the standalone-only `ID`/`Base`/`Name`/`Category` are optional on it). Constructors `TARDIS:NewInterior()`/`NewExterior()` (`---@return`-typed, like `NewPart`) type definition tables without literal-checking.
    - **Constructors.** `TARDIS:NewInterior()` / `TARDIS:NewExterior()` mirror `NewPart()` (`---@return` the typed table). A `---@return`-typed value is *not* literal-checked downstream, so `local T = TARDIS:NewInterior(); T.Interior = {...}` types the definition table for free. Convert `local T = {}` (incrementally-built) to the constructor. The registration-fn *params* (`AddInterior`/`AddExterior`/`AddInteriorTemplate`/`AddTipStyle`/...) ARE now strictly typed as their schema class (convention updated 2026-07-05). Partial definition literals are made to satisfy the type by routing through the constructor: build incrementally off `local E = TARDIS:NewExterior()`, or for a one-shot inline literal wrap it in a passthrough (`AddInteriorTemplate(id, TARDIS:NewInteriorTemplate({...}))`). A bare `local E = {...}` passed straight to the typed param still floods `param-type-mismatch`, so convert it. Do **not** build a parallel raw/loose class hierarchy to accept partials — it cascades through every nested type; the constructor is the clean escape.
    - **Type containers at the declaration, not each access.** `---@type Panel[]` on the `pages` local, `---@type table<string, table<string, string>>` on `TARDIS.LanguageCache`. Indexing a *typed* `table<K,V>` or `T[]` returns a **non-nil** `V` — so the RC2 "index is nilable" intuition was wrong; those fires came from *untyped* containers, and typing the container is the clean fix (no per-access annotation).
    - **Guaranteed-valid links** are typed non-nil: `gmod_tardis_interior.exterior` is `gmod_tardis` (an interior always has its exterior). Keep `gmod_tardis.interior` nilable — `exterior.interior` is not guaranteed.
    - **Fallbacks where the analyzer can't follow** (rare): the stdlib `table.Copy` is `---@return table` and *erases* the class → `---@type <class>` on the receiving local (`moved`/`rotated`, `MergeMetadata`'s `copy`). `TARDIS:CopyTable` is generic (`---@return T`) so a typed source needs no annotation (`base.lua`'s `E`, `CreateExteriorMetadata`'s `metadata`, and the tip in `InitializeTips` once `tip_styles`/`GetTipStyle` are typed `tardis_tip` and the copy uses `CopyTable`). Don't make the global `table.Copy` generic — it leaks input nilability and regresses `sh_versions`; a getter's *param* is safe to strictly type once its callers pass through a constructor — `AddTipStyle(style)` is typed `tardis_tip` now that the four style defs route through `TARDIS:NewTipStyle()` (a direct partial literal would still flood, so type the storage table/return instead in that case). `ipairs` loses *inherited-field overrides* on the element type — iterating `gmod_tardis_interior[]` (whose `exterior` overrides the inherited `gmod_door_interior.exterior`) makes `ent.exterior` resolve nilable, so re-assert with a `---@type gmod_tardis` local in the loop body (artron). (The return spelling doesn't matter — `table<number, T>` behaves the same; only a numeric `t[i]` index recovers the override, but the idiomatic `k,v` `ipairs` + the one annotation is preferred.) Plain (non-overridden) element fields survive `ipairs` fine — `tip` from `ipairs(interior.tips)` is correctly `tardis_tip`. `ColorAlpha` is stubbed `---@return table` and a stub return type can't be overridden from `.luatypes` (both an `@overload` and a full re-declaration lose to the stub — tested, so no override is kept for it), so its result needs `---@type Color` where a method like `:Unpack()` is called on it (the tip draw colors). `ENT:AddHook`'s `func` param is typed `fun(self: gmod_tardis_interior, ...)` (interior) / `fun(self: gmod_tardis, ...)` (exterior), so every hook callback's `self` is auto-typed - no per-callback `---@param self`. Tightening `self` this way surfaced latent type gaps it had been hiding (`Sounds.Hum` is a table not a string, `SequenceSpeed*` is `number|table`, `SetTracking`'s `ent`/`ply` are optional) - all since fixed. Only the non-AddHook override-table wrappers (`["Initialize"]={function(self)` in `sh_parts.lua`) still need `---@param self <class>` on a line break before `function`. Genuinely-nullable runtime fields (set on demand, e.g. `dematfailsound`, `CloisterLoop`) use `local x = self.foo; if x then` so the guard narrows the local.
- `need-check-nil` — **enabled.** Was 198 hints (hint-severity, so CI passed, but LSP noise), zero real bugs. It is the strictest rule: it only honours narrowing (`if x then`, `IsValid(x)`, `assert`) on values whose type is *declared* — values *inferred* from runtime assignments (cross-file `self.interior = e`, `self.foo = {}`) don't carry narrowing, which was the bulk of the noise. Fixes, in preference order:
    - **Declare runtime entity fields on the class** (the biggest win): `gmod_tardis.interior`/`pilot`/`LeakedInteriorHums`/`environment`, `gmod_tardis_interior.idlesounds`/`dematfailsound`/`spacebuild_env`. Existing `IsValid(self.field)` guards then narrow with no call-site changes — `IsValid()` *does* narrow a declared field (positive-block and early-return both), it just can't narrow an inferred one.
    - **Type the message-callback `self`.** `ENT:OnMessage`'s `func` param is typed `fun(self: gmod_tardis, ...)` / `fun(self: gmod_tardis_interior, ...)` (mirrors `AddHook`), so OnMessage handlers' `self.metadata`/`self.interior` resolve. The handler *storage* local is typed `table<string, function>` so the generic-`Entity` dispatch site (`messagehandlers[name](ent, …)`) doesn't trip `param-type-mismatch`.
    - **Finish the metadata schema** — make a field non-optional where `base.lua` always defines it (`Timings` + its fields, `Chameleon.AnimTime`, `LockedDoor.AnimPos`/`AnimTime`, teleport `DematInterruptSpeed`/`PrematDelay`/`PrematDelayFast`, `LightOverride.transitionspeed`, `Light.dynamicsize`, `ProjectedLight.baselightmix`, `DoorAnimationTime`, `TipSettings`+`tardis_tip` `view_range_min`/`max`); type the teleport sequences `number[]` so indexing is non-nil. (Safe because definition tables go through `NewInterior`/`NewExterior` and aren't literal-checked — verify with a full scan that no `missing-fields` appears.)
    - **The glua_ls loop-var bug**: a field or index of a `pairs`/`ipairs` loop variable reads nilable inside the loop body, and extracting it to a local makes it worse. `---@cast <loopvar> <type>` at the loop top resets it (see `cl_menuoptions`, `cl_tips`, `gmod_time_distortion_generator`, `sh_screen_ui`). A direct one-level use (`b.field`) is fine; the bug bites the extract-to-local and nested-index cases.
    - **Guard unguarded but invariant-safe derefs** (`local x = self.foo; if not IsValid(x) then return end`); De Morgan `if not (a and b)` → `if not a or not b` (a negated *parenthesized* compound doesn't narrow); a guard on an upvalue captured in a closure doesn't carry — re-extract a local inside the closure (`sh_redecorate`, `gmod_tardis_debug_pointer`). `---@cast`/`@as` with a comment only where re-checking is genuinely redundant (e.g. an `ent.TardisExterior` check ⇒ `gmod_tardis`).
  Third-party Spacebuild env types live in `.luatypes/spacebuild.lua` (`sb_resource_environment`/`sb_environment`).

When enabling a previously-disabled rule, prefer source-side type annotations over per-callsite asserts or `---@cast` directives — annotations propagate automatically; callsite workarounds need to be repeated everywhere.

- Entity-lookup helpers (`TARDIS:GetInteriorEnt`, `GetExteriorEnt`, etc.) return `gmod_tardis_interior?` / `gmod_tardis?` — those classes are defined in each SENT's `shared.lua`.
- `TARDIS:CopyTable` is `---@generic T; ---@param t T; ---@return T`, so it preserves the input's class — a typed source (`base.lua`'s `E` from `T.Exterior`, `CreateExteriorMetadata`'s `metadata` once `ExteriorsMetadata` is `table<string, tardis_exterior_metadata>`) flows through with no `---@type`. Do **not** give the *global* `table.Copy` the same generic via an override: it leaks the input's nilability through every call site (`table.Copy(optionalField)` → nilable), which regressed `sh_versions`. The stdlib `table.Copy` stays `---@return table`, and its sites with a typed source keep a `---@type <class>` local instead.
- The analyzer narrows nullability on locals, not on repeated field accesses. `if self.X[id] == nil then return end` doesn't narrow a later `self.X[id]` — extract to a local (`local raw = self.X[id]`) so the nil check narrows it.
- `assert(...)` is appropriate for runtime invariants where failure indicates a bug (GMod core lookups like `FindMetaTable("Player")`, paired-companion variables, post-bounds-check random picks). It is *not* a substitute for missing type annotations.
- `---@cast` and `--[[@as X]]` are last-resort workarounds for genuine analyzer limitations (e.g. `Entity:IsPlayer()` not narrowing to `Player`, runtime polymorphism). Before adding one, check if the issue is actually missing source-side type info — annotating a local table, a helper's return type, or propagating a class declaration is almost always the better fix and pays forward to other callsites. If the cast is needed, leave a one-line comment naming the analyzer quirk.
- When declaring a class (e.g. `---@class gmod_tardis_part : Entity`), only add `---@field` lines for properties the analyzer is currently failing to resolve. Speculative field lists make the class look more "documented" but introduce false `need-check-nil` hints (every `Entity?` field forces narrowing) and rot when the framework changes. Add fields one at a time, only when omitting them surfaces a real warning.
- Define Derma panel hook overrides (`Think`/`PerformLayout`/`Paint`/`OnChange`...) with `:` method syntax (`function panel:PerformLayout(w, h)`), not `panel.PerformLayout = function(self, w, h)`. For a method-typed field the analyzer binds `self` separately, so an explicit `self` in a `.`-assignment is one param too many (`redundant-parameter`); `:` makes it implicit. The analyzer reports these one at a time, so fixing one surfaces the next.

The `disable` list is empty and every rule is enforced; this section is now the reference for keeping it that way, not a migration to finish.

## Build / publish

No local build step - GMod loads `lua/` in place. CI publishes to three Steam Workshop items via the shared `gmod-addon-tools/publish-workshop.yml` reusable workflow:

- **Beta (`2649683927`)** - every push to `main` (after GLua Check passes) republishes the beta item from `ci.yml`'s `publish` job: silent login, `mfa: false`. The "latest `main`" channel.
- **Alpha (`2650203837`)** - pushes to the `alpha` branch publish the alpha item through the *same* `publish` job the same silent way (`mfa: false`); the `setup` job just resolves `vars.ALPHA_WORKSHOP_ID` instead of beta. Also reachable via `ci.yml`'s `workflow_dispatch` (`addon: alpha`, `publish: true`). The branch is **currently dormant** - it does not exist on origin - but the path stays wired for it to be resurrected; recreating the branch is all that's needed.
- **Stable (`307175678`)** - publishing a full GitHub **release** fires `release.yml` (same reusable workflow, `mfa: true`, phone-gated Steam Guard). A **bare tag ships nothing** (the trigger is `release: published`, not the tag) - cut a release to publish stable.

**Versioning.** There is no `version.json` to bump - the version comes from the git tag, so the tag you pick when cutting a release *is* `TARDIS.Version`. Beta/alpha builds report the last release they descend from, plus channel/commits/sha in `TARDIS.Build` (`TARDIS:GetBuildString()` renders e.g. `2025.5.0-beta.47+g1a2b3c4`). Release tags must be plain `X.Y.Z`; the legacy `release-NN-*` tags are deliberately excluded by the generator's `--match`.

**Change notes.** The Steam note for a stable release is composed from the release body: the **first paragraph** of a `## Summary` section becomes the note (Steam is BBCode, not Markdown - keep it plain prose), under an automatic version link. Beta/alpha builds get a commit link instead (no `## Summary`). Draft releases from `RELEASE_TEMPLATE.md`.

Test without shipping via `release.yml`'s `workflow_dispatch`: `dry_run: true` (real pack + Steam login, skip upload) or `tag: <version>` (preview - log the composed note only, no login). Note `dry_run` still fires the phone (the Steam Guard step gates on `mfa`, not `dry_run`); use `tag:` for a phone-free note preview.

<!-- >>> GENERATED shared conventions (gmod-addon-tools) - do not edit; regen: scripts/generate-claude-md.ps1 >>> -->

_Shared conventions for my GMod addons - generated from [`gmod-addon-tools/docs/gmod-addon-conventions.md`](https://github.com/AmyJeanes/gmod-addon-tools/blob/main/docs/gmod-addon-conventions.md). Edit it there, not in this file; the block below is overwritten by CI. Addon-specific guidance lives outside the markers._

## Code style

- **Pure Lua syntax only - no GMod-Lua extensions.** No `//` comments, no `continue`, no `!=`, no `&&`/`||`. Use `--`, `goto skip` (`continue` is a reserved word even as a `goto` label, so `goto continue` fails at load), `~=`, `and`/`or`.
- **Comments: concise, the _why_ not the _what_.** A couple of lines at most; reserve length for genuinely non-obvious rationale and bias toward cutting - match the surrounding density, don't pad to essay length. Don't restate the code, don't explain it by what it replaced, and keep the _why_ self-contained (no pointers to external docs or fragile cross-file references). Keep comments ASCII: `->` not an arrow, a single spaced hyphen for a dash (never a double `--`, which reads as a second comment marker, nor an em-dash).
- **Drop the loop variable you don't use** rather than naming it: `for _, v in pairs(t)`, `for k in pairs(t)`, `for _ = 1, n do`. The `unused` lint is on - keep the noise floor at zero.
- **Every `---@diagnostic disable` needs a paired reason** on the same or preceding line naming _why_ the rule is suppressed. The default is to fix the issue, not suppress it.

## First-time setup (before touching `.lua` files)

The tooling (`glua_check`, `glua_ls`, the GLua API stubs, and the wiki/typing type-model) is provisioned by the shared [`gmod-addon-tools`](https://github.com/AmyJeanes/gmod-addon-tools) module, cloned **beside this addon**. `scripts/install-tools.ps1` is a thin wrapper - `scripts/bootstrap.ps1` resolves the sibling module and it calls `Initialize-GmodTools`, so the version pins live once in the module and every addon runs the exact same engine.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/AmyJeanes/gmod-addon-tools ../gmod-addon-tools
pwsh -File scripts/install-tools.ps1
```

It is idempotent - re-running is a no-op when the pinned versions are already present, so it is also the recovery path when diagnostics look wrong. After a fresh install, run `/reload-plugins` so Claude Code re-launches the LSP against the new binary.

## Claude Code LSP integration (`glua-lsp` plugin)

Diagnostics, hover, and jump-to-definition come from the [`glua-lsp` plugin](https://github.com/AmyJeanes/gmod-claude-plugins) (marketplace `AmyJeanes/gmod-claude-plugins`), which wraps the [`glua_ls`](https://github.com/Pollux12/gmod-glua-ls) server - the same EmmyLua-Analyzer-Rust engine as `glua_check`, running long-lived. Diagnostics arrive automatically after every edit; no hook involvement. `.claude/settings.json` declares the marketplace so contributors get prompted to install on first open, and the plugin auto-resolves `glua_ls` from this project's `.tools/bin/` at launch (no global install, no PATH plumbing). The `glua-lsp:install-glua-ls` skill covers the same recovery flow if symptoms appear later. Treat reported diagnostics as actionable only if your edit caused them - pre-existing noise on unrelated lines is not in scope for the current change.

## Whole-repo scans (`scripts/glua-check.ps1`)

`glua_ls` only analyzes files as they are opened or edited. To audit the whole repo at once, run `pwsh -File scripts/glua-check.ps1` - it provisions tooling on demand (no-op when present) and runs `glua_check --warnings-as-errors` against the workspace root. It takes no path filter, so it always scans everything; CI runs the same script. Useful after a fix ripples across the tree, or when picking the project up to surface latent issues the LSP hasn't opened yet.

**Local (Windows) vs CI (Linux) can diverge - CI is authoritative.** The same tooling and files can flag differently on Linux and no `.luarc.json` change closes that platform gap, so a green local `glua-check` isn't conclusive - watch CI's Linux `GLua Check` job after pushing typing changes. Most often the culprit is a strict `---@class`/`---@type` on a *partial or reused literal* (passes Windows, fails Linux); use `table`/`table[]?` or a `--[[@as Class]]` cast instead of annotating the literal.

## Typing enforcement (`scripts/typing-check.ps1`)

`glua_check` catches _wrong_ types but not _missing_ ones - an untyped param is a silent `any` it never flags. `Test-GmodTyping` (CI: `typing-check.yml`) closes that gap, failing the build on any of: an untyped param, annotation rot (a `---@param` for a param that no longer exists), a modeled function whose resolved return type contains `unknown`, a hook fire-site argument that resolves to `unknown`, or a `:CallHook`-style hook whose receiver resolves to `unknown` (so its "Fired on" column would render _Unknown_ - usually fixed with a `---@param self <class>` on the enclosing function). Satisfy it at the **source** - prefer a `---@param` / `---@return` / `---@class` annotation over a per-callsite `---@cast`, since annotations propagate to every caller. The only accepted escapes are explicit and greppable: `---@param x any` (a reviewed, genuine `any`), an `_` discard for a deliberately-unused arg, and a file-level `---@vendored` marker on third-party code.

Where an addon fires its own hooks, callback payload params are typed by a generated `---@overload` catalogue (`scripts/generate-hook-types.ps1`, CI: `generate-hook-types.yml`) - do not hand-edit it; retype a payload at its `CallHook` / `hook.Run` site instead. Custom global-hook overloads are spliced into the provisioned `hook.lua` by `Initialize-GmodTools`, so after pulling a change to a generated fragment mid-session, re-run `scripts/install-tools.ps1` (it re-syncs) then `/reload-plugins` to refresh live types.

## Bumping the shared tooling

Tool versions and this conventions block are pinned to a `gmod-addon-tools` tag. Bump the version constants in `gmod-addon-tools/src/install.ps1` (or edit the shared docs); merging to the module's `main` auto-cuts a new tag, and Renovate then raises a pin-bump PR here that regenerates the affected artifacts and runs GLua Check before it merges. CI pins the module by tag (the `ref:` in each workflow); a local sibling checkout uses whatever branch it is on, so keep it on the pinned tag to mirror CI exactly.
